info.title: MarketX API
info.description: Social commerce API. Auth via `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>`; see docs/API_STRUCTURE.md for the route map and token lifecycle.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Store slug — the URL handle (e.g. `hadronpower`), not an id.

404.description: Seller profile not found (no active store with that slug)

get.description: Despite the `{id}` path name, this expects the **store slug** (e.g. `hadronpower`), NOT a UUID. Public: returns only active stores; the owner (authenticated) also sees their own inactive store. See also `GET /seller/by-slug/{slug}`.

post.description: Step 1 of inline checkout auth — emails a 6-digit one-time code.
Step 2 of inline checkout auth — verifies the 6-digit code, auto-registers new users, issues tokens (body + httpOnly cookies).
Sends a reset link if the email exists. Always returns 200 with an identical message — no account enumeration.
Authenticates a user. Returns tokens in the body **and** sets httpOnly cookies. Native clients read the body and store both tokens in Keystore-backed encrypted storage.

post.summary: Log out / revoke the current session
post.description: Revokes the session tied to the refresh-token cookie and clears auth cookies. Native clients should additionally discard their stored tokens.

parameters.name: redirectTo
parameters.description: Relative path to land on after login.

500.description: Provider not configured on the server

get.description: Redirects (302) to the provider consent screen and sets a `state` cookie. Browser-only flow — native clients should use a provider SDK with PKCE.

302.description: Redirect into the app (or to an error page)

get.description: Provider redirect target. Validates `state` against the httpOnly cookie (rejects mismatch), provisions/links the account, sets auth cookies, and redirects back into the app. Not called directly by clients.

parameters.name: X-Refresh-Token
parameters.description: Native transport. Omit when sending the cookie (web).

401.description: Missing, invalid, expired, or revoked refresh token

get.description: Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token. The refresh token rotates on every call — the one you send is immediately invalidated. **Web:** sends the httpOnly `refreshToken` cookie; response returns only `accessToken` (rotated token stays in the cookie). **Native:** sends the token via `X-Refresh-Token` header or `{ refreshToken }` body; response returns `{ accessToken, refreshToken }` — persist the new refresh token.

post.summary: Register a user + store in one atomic flow
post.description: Creates a `seller` account and its store, then auto-logs-in (tokens in body + httpOnly cookies). Store slug: lowercase `[a-z0-9-]`, 3+ chars, not reserved.

400.description: Invalid input (e.g. passwords do not match)

post.description: Creates a `user` account and sends an email-verification link. Username: 3–30 chars, `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`. Password must meet complexity rules.
Consumes a single-use reset token, sets the new password, and revokes all existing sessions. The user must log in again afterwards.
Public. Responds identically whether or not the account exists / is already verified — no account enumeration.

get.summary: Read tokens from OAuth cookies (web only)
get.description: Web OAuth bridge: hands the httpOnly tokens set by the OAuth callback back to client JS. **Native clients must not use this** — do native OAuth with PKCE and receive tokens directly instead.
